_comment: Trap houses let you stash product off your person (frees carry) and have RPG employees PUSH it remotely while you're elsewhere. Setup cost is one-time to establish the operation in a city. storage_lb is how much product it holds; slots is how many employees can work it. Employees are RPG units: push_per_day = grams sold per in-game day, skim = fraction they pocket, heat = heat they draw. In-game day = 6 real minutes (DAY_SECONDS in trap.gd) so a passive operation pays off across a play session and while away.

house_tiers.name: Stash spot
house_tiers.note: A rented room with a hollow door. Holds a little, one worker.

house_tiers.name: Trap house
house_tiers.note: A whole unit off the books. Real volume, two hands.

house_tiers.name: Stash house
house_tiers.note: A quiet house on a quiet street. Moves weight, crew of four.

employees.name: Corner kid
employees.note: Young, hungry, sloppy. Pockets a little, draws eyes.

employees.name: Veteran slinger
employees.note: Done this for years. Quiet, steady, loyal-ish.

employees.name: Lieutenant
employees.note: Runs the spot like clockwork. Barely skims.
